content: Drawing on the lexical origin of reflexion as both linguistic reflection in human language learning and optical reflection of light, we can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a linguistic 'mirror' that borrows the core dual meaning of reflexion from human vocabulary learning to address a key gap in current multi-perspective LLM reflection research. This dual framing lets us design a new reflexion benchmark that tests whether LLMs can not only reflect on their own reasoning errors, but also reflect the lexical and conceptual nuance of reflexion itself back to human developers, revealing hidden alignment gaps between how humans and LLMs understand self-evaluation.
